documentation.readme: > Blackbird is a powerful OSINT tool that combines fast username and email searches across more than 600 platforms with free AI-powered profiling. By leveraging community-driven projects like WhatsMyName, it ensures low false positive rates and high-quality results. Features include smart filters, polished PDF/CSV exports, and fully automated analysis — all from a single CLI.
usage_sections: Usage: Search by username Search by email Export results to PDF

documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: > Blackbird is a powerful OSINT tool that combines fast username and email searches across more than 600 platforms with free AI-powered profiling. By leveraging community-driven projects like WhatsMyName, it ensures low false positive rates and high-quality results. Features include smart filters, polished PDF/CSV exports, and fully automated analysis — all from a single CLI.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/p1ngul1n0/blackbird; pip install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python blackbird.py --username johndoe`. Further invocations from upstream: `python blackbird.py --email johndoe@example.com`; `python blackbird.py --email  --pdf`. > Blackbird is a powerful OSINT tool that combines fast username and email searches across more than 600 platforms with free AI-powered profiling. By leveraging community-driven projects like WhatsMyName, it ensures low false positive rates and high-quality results. Features include smart filters, polished PDF/CSV expo.

name: Blackbird
summary: > Blackbird is a powerful OSINT tool that combines fast username and email searches across more than 600 platforms with free AI-powered profiling. By leveraging community-driven projects like WhatsMyName, it ensures low false positive rates and high-quality results. Features include smart filters, polished PDF/CSV exports, and fully automated analysis — all from a single CLI.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need osint, use Blackbird because > Blackbird is a powerful OSINT tool that combines fast username and email searches across more than 600 platforms with free AI-powered profiling. By leveraging.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python blackbird.py --username johndoe` as documented upstream for Blackbird.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python blackbird.py --email johndoe@example.com` as documented upstream for Blackbird.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python blackbird.py --email  --pdf` as documented upstream for Blackbird.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python blackbird.py --setup-ai` as documented upstream for Blackbird.
When chaining osint tools, pair Blackbird with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Blackbird --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
